question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAndIncrementLogIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes two threads to concurrently process the same RecordItem (duplicate delivery in parallel), causing getAndIncrementLogIndex() to initialize logIndex as a new AtomicInteger(0) in both threads before either sets the field, resulting in duplicate log index values 0 being assigned to different contract logs?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAndIncrementLogIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a contract transaction with an extremely large number of log entries during a network partition that causes the RecordItem to be re-processed, causing getAndIncrementLogIndex() to continue incrementing from where it left off (since logIndex is @NonFinal and @Setter), producing non-contiguous log indices in the database?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: isChild()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction with hasParentConsensusTimestamp() returning true but with a parentConsensusTimestamp that equals its own consensusTimestamp during a network partition, causing isChild() to return true while parseParent() returns null (no matching previous), creating an inconsistent state where the transaction is classified as a child but has no parent reference?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: parseParent()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that drops the immediate previous RecordItem but delivers a sibling with a parent reference, causing parseParent() to return previous.parent (the sibling's parent) as the current item's parent even when the timestamps don't semantically match, due to the shallow two-level lookup not verifying the full ancestry chain?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: build()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that delivers a RecordItem with a TransactionRecord whose receipt status is SUCCESS_BUT_MISSING_EXPECTED_OPERATION, causing parseSuccess() to return true and the item to be processed as successful, while downstream handlers that don't check for this specific status code incorrectly apply state changes that should have been conditional?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: parseTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction using the deprecated transaction.hasBody() path (legacy format) during a network partition that causes the importer to switch between parsing modes, causing parseTransaction() to use the deprecated body field on replay but signedTransactionBytes on first parse, producing two RecordItems with different signatureMaps for the same consensusTimestamp?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: parsePossiblyHookContractRelatedParent()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction with contractNum == HOOK_CONTRACT_NUM (365) as the current transaction's contract result during a network partition, causing parsePossiblyHookContractRelatedParent() to skip the entire hook parent search (due to the != HOOK_CONTRACT_NUM guard), leaving hookParent as null and causing hook storage changes to be processed without proper hook context?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: addEntityId()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes entityTransactionPredicate to remain as REJECT_ALL (the default) when a RecordItem is processed before setEntityTransactionPredicate() is called, causing addEntityId() to silently drop all entity transaction records for that item, resulting in missing EntityTransaction entries in the database?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: addNftTransactionEntityId()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that delivers an NFT transfer RecordItem before setEntityNftTransactionPredicate() is called, causing addNftTransactionEntityId() to use the default REJECT_ALL predicate and silently drop all NFT entity transaction records, resulting in incomplete NFT transfer history in the database?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: parseParent()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a batch transaction with inner transactions during a network partition that reorders the batch children, causing the grandparent lookup path (previous.parent.parent) in parseParent() to return null when previous.parent.parent is null (only two levels deep), silently dropping the parent link for deeply nested batch children?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: isTopLevel()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction with transactionNonce == 0 and hasParentConsensusTimestamp() == true during a network partition, causing isTopLevel() to return true (nonce == 0 branch short-circuits) even though the transaction has a parent, leading to the child being processed as a top-level transaction and its effects being applied twice?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getContractTransactions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes concurrent access to the contractTransactions field (which is @NonFinal and lazily initialized without synchronization), causing a race condition where two threads both see null and both initialize the HashMap, with one thread's ContractTransaction entries being lost?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getEntityTransactions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes concurrent access to the entityTransactions field (lazily initialized HashMap without synchronization in getEntityTransactions()), causing a race condition where concurrent addEntityId() calls from different threads produce a corrupted HashMap with missing entries?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: parseSuccess()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that delivers a child transaction whose parent field is set to a RecordItem from a previous (unrelated) record file with isSuccessful() == false, causing parseSuccess() to incorrectly mark the child as failed, suppressing legitimate state changes for a successful child transaction?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: parseTransactionType()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TransactionBody with multiple unknown fields (size != 1) during a network partition that causes re-parsing, causing parseTransactionType() to log an error with the full hex-encoded body bytes (potentially containing sensitive data) and return DATA_NOT_SET, leaking transaction content through log aggregation systems accessible to unprivileged users?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: build()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a TransactionID whose AccountID is empty/default during a network partition that drops the account resolution, causing EntityId.of(transactionBody.getTransactionID().getAccountID()) in build() to return EntityId.EMPTY as payerAccountId, which is then stored in all ContractTransaction and EntityTransaction records for that item?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: parsePossiblyHookContractRelatedParent()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that delivers hook child transactions with a previous chain that cycles back (due to corrupted previous references), causing the while loop in parsePossiblyHookContractRelatedParent() to run indefinitely since the termination condition 'candidateRecord != parent' may never be true if parent is null?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: nextHookContext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that delivers a child RecordItem with hookExecutionQueue == null and parent.hookExecutionQueue also null, causing nextHookContext() to recursively call parent.nextHookContext() which also returns null, and then processHookStorageChanges() logs a recoverable error but continues processing, causing hook storage changes to be silently dropped for all affected children?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: parseTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction where signedTransactionBytes contains a valid SignedTransaction but the nested bodyBytes contain a protobuf message that is valid bytes but parses to a TransactionBody with a different transaction type than what the TransactionRecord receipt indicates, exploiting the lack of cross-validation between parsed body and record during network partition replay?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: addContractTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a contract transaction referencing thousands of distinct EntityIds during a network partition that causes the RecordItem to be processed multiple times, causing the contractTransactions HashMap to accumulate entries across multiple processing runs (since it is @NonFinal and not reset between runs), leading to an inflated contractIds list in populateContractTransactions()?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: isInvalidIdError()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction that returns INVALID_NODE_ACCOUNT_ID during a network partition that causes the mirror node to re-process the item, causing isInvalidIdError() to return true and downstream handlers to skip entity creation, while on the second processing run the same check returns true again, resulting in permanently missing entity records for a transaction that should have been retried?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: parseParent()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that delivers two transactions with identical consensusTimestamp values (timestamp collision), causing parseParent() to match the wrong one as the parent when comparing parentTimestamp.equals(previous.transactionRecord.getConsensusTimestamp()), leading to an incorrect parent-child relationship being persisted?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: build()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes a RecordItem to be built with a transactionRecord from one record file and a transaction from a different record file (due to stream interleaving), causing build() to compute a consensusTimestamp from the mismatched record while the transactionBody belongs to a different transaction, producing a RecordItem with inconsistent fields?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: parseTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with signedTransactionBytes that is non-empty but fails SignedTransaction.parseFrom() with InvalidProtocolBufferException during a network partition that corrupts the stream, causing parseTransaction() to throw a ProtobufException that halts the entire record file processing and blocks all subsequent transactions in that file from being ingested?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getTransactionStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TransactionRecord with a receipt that has an unrecognized status value during a network partition that delivers a future protocol version, causing getTransactionStatus() to return an integer that doesn't map to any known ResponseCodeEnum, which is then stored as the transaction result and causes incorrect filtering in downstream status-based queries?
